item.title: Most people are thinking about aging all wrong
item.description: A new study found that physical and cognitive gains in later life are surprisingly common.

item.title: The ‘deep state’ didn’t take down Nixon. But why did Vance say it did?
item.description: Courts, Congress and the press drove Watergate. And such a scandal could happen again.

item.title: What ‘Almost heaven, West Virginia’ has to do with you
item.description: “Country Roads” is West Virginia’s apolitical yet patriotic cultural triumph.

item.title: I chaired the FEC. Hawaii’s attack on Citizens United is dangerous.
item.description: A new law in the Aloha State bars most organizations from speaking on political issues.

item.title: I’m a professor at Berkeley. Bring back this requirement for entry.
item.description: The outcomes of California’s admissions experiment are even worse than predicted.

item.title: The straitjacket
item.description: Michael Ramirez cartoon on the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: The keys to your life savings might be compromised
item.description: China is deploying market access to steal intellectual property, monitor Americans and conduct economic espionage.

item.title: Trump’s ‘small piece of the puzzle’ in the Iran war is the whole game
item.description: With Hezbollah still in Lebanon, the Iranian revolution lives on, and so does the conflict.

item.title: What a ‘mansion tax’ did to Los Angeles
item.description: Taxes that narrowly target the rich are not the best way for cities to broadly help the poor.

item.title: Trump wanted to break the system. The system is breaking him instead.
item.description: The courts, Congress and the Constitution are doing what a weak opposition could not.

item.title: A reset on civil rights upends DEI — and the ‘New Right’
item.description: Will the president’s legacy lie in upholding the spirit of civil rights law?

item.title: Pete Hegseth’s purges claim one of the military’s superstars
item.description: Gen. Christopher Donahue heads into early retirement as the defense secretary seeks to root out MAGA disloyalty.

item.title: This voter ID law would spell trouble from Florida to Texas
item.description: The Save America Act has a worthy goal. But states can run elections on their own.
